question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the preparsedDocumentProvider cache to serve a stale cached document that references an outdated freeze-key or account state, causing an unfreeze operation to be applied to the wrong account version and leaving the target account permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a JSON number with exactly 19 digits (maxNumberLength(19)) that represents a value exceeding Long.MAX_VALUE, causing silent numeric overflow in account ID or token ID parsing, resulting in an unfreeze being applied to a non-existent account while the real account remains frozen?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a JSON number with 20 digits (exceeding maxNumberLength(19)) in an unfreeze request, causing Jackson to throw a StreamConstraintsException that results in a 500 error, while the freeze state is never rolled back and funds remain permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: static{}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gap between maxCharacters(10000) in ParserOptions and maxDocumentLength(11000) in Jackson StreamReadConstraints to craft a document that passes Jackson deserialization but is rejected by the GraphQL parser, causing an unfreeze operation to be silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user send a query where a @constraint directive on an account ID field fails (e.g., negative shard number), triggering RETURN_NULL on the freeze-status field resolver, so the API reports the account as having null freeze status instead of frozen, causing the caller to skip the unfreeze step?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user register a conflicting scalar by exploiting the order of wiringBuilder directives in runtimeWiringConfigurer(), overriding the GraphQlDuration or GraphQlTimestamp scalar coercion so that freeze-expiry timestamps are parsed incorrectly, causing a freeze to appear permanent when it should have expired?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a deeply nested JSON object (depth > 10, maxNestingDepth(10)) as a GraphQL variables payload, causing Jackson to reject the request at the HTTP layer before GraphQL processing, so an unfreeze mutation is never executed and funds remain frozen?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MaxQueryDepthInstrumentation(10) counts fragment spreads toward depth, crafting a query with inline fragments that push the depth over 10 and abort an unfreeze operation mid-execution, leaving funds frozen?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a batch of GraphQL operations where one operation's complexity pushes the ChainedInstrumentation total over MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation(200), causing the entire batch including an unfreeze operation to be aborted, while the freeze operation from a prior batch remains in effect?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: static{}] Can an unprivileged external user send a query with exactly 1001 tokens (exceeding maxTokens(1000)) where the 1001st token is part of an unfreeze argument, causing the parser to abort after parsing the freeze portion but before the unfreeze argument, leaving the account in a frozen state?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query where a validation directive on a nested freeze-status field returns null via RETURN_NULL, causing the parent resolver to receive null and propagate it upward as 'account not found', masking the frozen state and preventing the caller from initiating an unfreeze?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ChainedInstrumentation to cause a partial execution where MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation passes but MaxQueryDepthInstrumentation fails after the freeze resolver has already written state, leaving the account frozen without the corresponding unfreeze being applied?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests that race to populate the CachedPreparsedDocumentProvider cache with a malformed unfreeze document, causing a cache stampede where the first cached error entry is served to all subsequent unfreeze requests, permanently blocking unfreeze for that query shape?
